{"step_by_step_thinking": "The term 'eburnation' in dentistry refers to a condition where the dentine undergoes a process similar to burning, resulting in a brown or black discoloration. This is typically seen in arrested caries, where the lesion has progressed to the point of mineral loss but has stabilized and is no longer actively demineralizing. None of the other types of caries (acute, chronic, or root caries) exhibit this specific characteristic.", "answer_choice": "C"}
